reasoning: The Lean statement captures both components of "P ∩ H is the unique Sylow p-subgroup of H": existence (Nonempty (Sylow p H)) and uniqueness together with the identification (every R : Sylow p H has R.toSubgroup = (H ⊓ P.toSubgroup).subgroupOf H). In Mathlib, subgroupOf is comap along the subtype inclusion, so (H ⊓ P.toSubgroup).subgroupOf H correctly represents P ∩ H viewed as a subgroup of H. Combined with Nonempty, the universal equality forces P ∩ H to itself be a Sylow p-subgroup and to be the unique one, matching the informal claim. The Fintype assumptions surface the implicit finiteness premise standard for the Dummit–Foote chapter 4 Sylow-theoretic setting and are required for the uniqueness argument to go through, so this is a faithful reading rather than narrowing.
